name: freshness-ribbon
description: Per-section last-content-review date badge on each section heading (right end, after [edit] pencil). Three-stop colour scale: fresh (green, ≤90 days) / stale (amber, 91–365 days) / archived (gray, >365 days). Data source: git-blame at section level (excluding whitespace-only commits) or frontmatter content_reviewed_on override. dateModified per-section WebPageElement JSON-LD emission.
html:
Background [edit] 2026-03-15

Current implementations [edit] 2024-09-12

Historical context [edit] 2022-11-04

freshness_stops.name: fresh
freshness_stops.description: ≤90 days since last substantive content change

freshness_stops.name: stale
freshness_stops.description: 91–365 days since last substantive content change

freshness_stops.name: archived
freshness_stops.description: >365 days since last substantive content change

data_sources.description: Most recent commit touching content lines in section; excludes whitespace-only changes (--ignore-all-space --ignore-blank-lines)
content_reviewed_on per-section frontmatter field; manual editor override

aria: Ribbon is <span> (not focusable) inside <h2>. Heading semantic is unbroken. aria-label on ribbon announces date AND semantic class ('Last reviewed YYYY-MM-DD — fresh/stale/archived'). Colour is never sole differentiator — date string is always present. :root[data-freshness-display='off'] hides all ribbons for reader-preference toggle.
ai_hint: Emits per-section dateModified on WebPageElement JSON-LD nodes. additionalType values: FreshSection / StaleSection / ArchivedSection. Threshold parameters documented in wiki llms.txt so external AI consumers know the substrate's thresholds.
engine_scope_note: Engine work (cluster-side, project-knowledge): section-boundary detection, git-blame at section level, frontmatter content_reviewed_on parsing, threshold computation, JSON-LD emission, ARIA-label generation.
open_questions: Reader-preference default: ribbons on by default OR off by default with toggle? On-by-default adds visual weight; off-by-default hides signal until activated.
Display format: ISO '2026-03-15' vs relative '6 weeks ago' vs seasonal 'Spring 2026'. Substrate ratifies.
